context.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 17?
context.market_title: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $72,000 on April 17?

decision.primary_crux: The move from 0.745 to 0.765 is best treated as repricing rather than a broken thesis: BTC is still more likely than not to close above $72,000 on the April 17 noon ET Binance minute, but the market is now further above the bounded fair-value range and the exact-minute downside-tail risk remains the decisive discount.
secondary_cruxes: The disagreement remains mostly about confidence calibration, not direction; Yes is still favored, but not at the market's updated level.
With no new governing-source evidence and only a small time change, this refresh mainly weakens any prior value case rather than changing the directional estimate.
Residual Binance UI-versus-API ambiguity is minor, but the larger issue is still ordinary crypto volatility landing on one exact settlement minute.

valuation.compression_reason: No new bounded evidence justifies changing the prior probability range; the refresh is primarily a market repricing against the same exact-minute settlement risk.

portfolio_constraints: Do not add fresh exposure at the updated market price.
Treat exact-minute Binance settlement risk as the dominant execution constraint even when spot remains above strike.
Reopen only if price cheapens materially or a fresher same-venue check near settlement improves the cushion.

bands.name: max_enter
bands.notes: Would require a much cheaper price and still a fresh Binance check near settlement.

bands.name: scaled_enter
bands.notes: Potential value zone only if same-venue price state remains comfortably above strike.

bands.name: hold
bands.notes: Bounded fair-value zone; maintain watch-only hold posture.

bands.name: trim
bands.notes: Above fair value for a one-minute settlement contract with meaningful path risk.

bands.name: exit
bands.notes: Avoid or exit at rich prices because exact-minute downside tail dominates residual certainty.

thesis_breakers: BTC falls back toward or below 72k on Binance before the Apr 17 fixing window.
A concrete downside macro or crypto catalyst emerges that materially raises the odds of a sub-72k settlement minute.
Evidence appears that Binance's operative settlement surface differs from the assumed UI/API equivalence.
time_breakers: A fresh direct Binance observation closer to Apr 17 12:00 ET should supersede this packet before any action.
The actual Apr 17 12:00 ET Binance 1-minute close fully resolves and obsoletes this judgment.
market_structure_breakers: Market reprices sharply again without corresponding same-venue spot support, changing the value comparison.
Binance operational or microstructure issues emerge near settlement.
reversal_conditions: Upgrade only if price becomes materially cheaper relative to fair value or if a fresh pre-fix Binance check shows a meaningfully larger cushion.
Downgrade if BTC loses cushion or if downside volatility or catalyst risk increases.
Do not auto-reverse from this packet.

key_uncertainties: The true probability of an ordinary 2-3% drawdown landing exactly at the settlement minute.
Whether the market embeds fresher venue-specific information than the bounded package contains.
How much residual UI-versus-API settlement-surface uncertainty matters in practice.
what_would_change_my_mind: A fresh Binance check near settlement showing sustained trade materially above 72k would move me closer to market.
A move back toward 72k or evidence of elevated downside catalyst risk would lower fair value materially.
A materially cheaper market price with unchanged Binance cushion would create a cleaner edge.
reasons_to_pass: The updated market price is now even further above bounded fair value.
This remains a single-minute single-venue settlement contract where path risk matters more than broad BTC direction.
The refresh added price movement, not new evidence that would justify raising probability.

audit.one_sentence_rationale: BTC is still more likely than not to finish above $72,000 on the April 17 Binance noon minute, but the market's move up to 0.765 only makes the previous below-market valuation view worse, so the disciplined output remains watch-only with fair value anchored near 0.71 rather than chasing a richer price.
audit.notes: This refresh is a pure valuation update: same directional Yes, weaker anti-market execution case.
